I'm still learning riffs and runs and would like some help please. I did two takes.0:100:10

thread.author: Daniel E
thread.text: This is really crazy how you took on a song like this with acapella. I’m not familiar with the song/artist but after hearing this part (sounds like the main chorus) I have just two commentsAs you build energy toward this part of the song, try and focus on voice control. Just because you reach the most energy of the song your mind auto-pilots to “I just sing loud/shout”. Focus on being as clear as possible.During higher registers of songs like this don’t be afraid to enter into your falsetto voic

thread.author: Shari Bennett-Jackson
thread.text: Shari Bennett-Jackson • Oct '24@Paul Farrales You're welcome. Got it. Your runs sound pretty good in this song but I do think there's opportunity to get them to sound cleaner. Agility usually is related to your tongue because the root of your tongue is connected to the top of the larynx. If you're singing and the back of the tongue is suppressed it will be difficult to have agility, but if it's up it is easier. I know this sounds silly but if you try to hiss like a cat on a pentatonic scale or w

There is an airy tone in general here and there

post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)130 votes

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)130 votes
